purpose: Receipt-only source leads for Telos as a mathematical, physical, rendering, and educator-facing creation engine.

mission.operator_note: Build AI and LLM workflows, including live-state models, that can assist mathematicians like Inigo Quilez, exceptional physicists and mathematicians, and educators who need advanced tools and demos.
telos_implications: Creative tools must expose formulas, transforms, parameters, visual output, and verification receipts in the same workspace.
Math and physics demos need replayable scene specs, measurement overlays, exportable artifacts, and readable failure states.
Live-state models should receive structured observations and tool results, not hidden context or unverifiable screenshots.
Educator surfaces should support explain, perturb, replay, compare, and export loops without losing provenance.

channel_leads.notes: Channel metadata observed through yt-dlp on 2026-06-28; use as a source lead and resolve individual claims through primary videos, articles, repositories, papers, or reproducible demos.

results.title: Coding a procedural brick tower
results.telos_adoption: Use as a model for formula-visible procedural construction where each stage can emit parameters, image receipts, and replay handles.

results.title: Will JEPA Fail & World Models are just Tools?
results.telos_adoption: Treat world-model claims as source leads for structured state, observation, and action receipts rather than as validated model-performance claims.

results.title: François Chollet: Keras, Deep Learning, and the Progress of AI
results.telos_adoption: Use as a lead for evaluating whether Telos demos test abstraction and generalization through perturbable tasks rather than static prompt recall.

results.title: DeepSeek V4 DeepSpec Is Here & A New GLM Model Matches Mythos!
results.telos_adoption: Keep model-release claims behind Crucible gates: current metadata is a source lead, not evidence of benchmark parity.

results.title: DeepSeek Just Made Every LLM Faster, For Free
results.telos_adoption: Use as an efficiency lead only after fresh primary-source and local benchmark receipts; Telos should separate performance claims from demo inspiration.

results.title: cuda-oxide: Pure Rust GEMM on NVIDIA Blackwell. Are we SoL yet?
results.telos_adoption: Use as a GPU-compute substrate lead for future local kernels, renderer benchmarks, and model/tool acceleration receipts.

results.title: Nathan Seiberg: Symmetry, Anomaly, Gauging, Proliferation, and Condensation
results.telos_adoption: Use as a high-end physics education lead: the engine should support diagrams, state transitions, invariants, and source-backed explanatory receipts.

results.title: The Thermodynamic AI Chip · Thomas Ahle
results.telos_adoption: Use as a hardware-and-physics lead for Telos performance models, local hardware scaling policy, and energy-aware renderer/model orchestration after primary-source and transcript receipts are gathered.

claim_boundaries: This ledger proves current metadata capture for source leads; it does not validate transcript accuracy, implementation correctness, benchmark claims, model-release claims, or scientific correctness.
Video titles and uploader metadata are not a substitute for primary papers, repositories, specifications, lecture notes, or reproduced demos.
Use these leads to shape Telos requirements and research queues; promote claims to MATCH only after Gather, Index, and Crucible can attach stronger evidence.
next_actions: gather.docs: resolve Inigo Quilez website, Shadertoy references, and relevant shader/math articles as lawful source receipts.
gather.arxiv: resolve physics, world-model, abstraction, and GPU-kernel papers behind the supplied videos.
gather.video: resolve the thermodynamic AI chip transcript and linked primary sources before promoting any hardware or energy-efficiency claims.
index.context: map render-nd, render-sound, sense-core, viable-viz, studio-engine organs, and studio-libs for educator-demo integration.
crucible.assess: add negative fixtures for unverifiable benchmark claims, missing formula provenance, hidden model state, and non-replayable demos.
